---
title: "Resources"
---

## Website Building

- This (somewhat Macalester-specific) [student resources document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/19wlevPT0p1WF7oG9ccFEN5MrRXJBK9zHuSkVSoel6v8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.713tjfdypqah){target="_blank"} and this [Quarto website guide](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1nNxCn8dGELeACHyRYQ0-IwT5QXd0XN0uEaBfhYR2ktM/edit?tab=t.0){target="_blank"}, both created by [Professor Brianna Heggeseth](https://bcheggeseth.github.io){target="_blank"}, first got me interested in creating a website.

- I had never used git or Github prior to this project. The UC Santa Barbara Bren School of Environmental Science and Management's [software installation guide](https://ucsb-meds.github.io/MEDS-installation-guide/){target="_blank"} was a great introduction to how to configure both.

- Now, working with Quarto, I'm constantly referencing [Sam Shanny-Csik's materials](https://samanthacsik.github.io){target="_blank"} - her resources are **so** approachable, thorough, and easy to understand:
  * [Creating your personal website using Quarto](https://ucsb-meds.github.io/creating-quarto-websites/){target="_blank"}
  * [Creating your own personal branding guide](https://ucsb-meds.github.io/EDS-296-DS-portfolios/course-materials/week2-customizing-websites.html#pre-class-prep){target="_blank"} (under the 'Pre-class prep' section)
  * [Customizing Quarto websites](https://ucsb-meds.github.io/customizing-quarto-websites/#/title-slide){target="_blank"} slideshow
